scenario: An Ad-hoc Subprocess for document-review has 5 inner tasks: review images, check signatures, verify dates, scan watermark, cross-reference DB. The reviewer should complete at least 3 before the Ad-hoc subprocess concludes.
question: Which configuration fits?

options.text: Set loopCardinality = 3 on the Ad-hoc Subprocess. The loopCardinality property belongs to Multi-Instance configuration and controls the number of parallel or sequential instances to create from a collection. Ad-hoc Subprocesses do not use loopCardinality; they use the completionCondition property to determine when the subprocess should terminate based on inner activity execution state. Setting loopCardinality on an Ad-hoc Subprocess has no effect in the Zeebe engine.

Use an Inclusive Gateway inside the subprocess with three outgoing paths for the required checks. Gateways route process flow; they do not determine when an Ad-hoc Subprocess as a whole terminates. An Inclusive Gateway could control which inner tasks start, but terminating the Ad-hoc Subprocess after any three tasks complete requires the completionCondition property on the Ad-hoc Subprocess element itself, not gateway configuration.

Ad-hoc Subprocesses always require all inner tasks to complete before the subprocess finishes. This is factually flawed. Ad-hoc Subprocesses in Camunda 8 support a completionCondition FEEL expression that is evaluated after each inner activity completes. When the condition returns true, any still-executing activities are cancelled and the Ad-hoc Subprocess finishes, enabling partial completion with fewer than all inner tasks having run.

Set the completionCondition on the Ad-hoc Subprocess -- a FEEL expression counting completed inner activities such that when three or more have finished the subprocess terminates. The FEEL expression evaluates after each inner activity completes; remaining active activities are cancelled when the condition becomes true. This directly matches the requirement of completing after at least three of five reviews are done.

a.text: Incorrect. Ad-hoc Subprocess uses completionCondition to determine when it terminates. FEEL expression has access to inner activity execution state. Counting completed activities >= 3 matches the requirement.

b.text: Incorrect. loopCardinality is Multi-Instance, not Ad-hoc.

c.text: Incorrect. Gateway routes flow, not Ad-hoc termination.

d.text: Correct. Ad-hoc supports partial completion via completionCondition.

explanation: Ad-hoc supports partial completion via completionCondition.
